compiled_state.title: GREEN: Implement archive eligibility traversal in ticket-graph.py
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

Add `compute_archive_eligible(tracker_dir)` function to `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-graph.py`:
1. Load all tickets via reduce_all_tickets (no archive filter — need to see all tickets)
2. Identify all open tickets (status != 'closed')
3. BFS/DFS from each open ticket following depends_on and blocks edges (bidirectional traversal on dep graph)
4. Mark all reachable tickets as ineligible
5. Return set of closed ticket IDs that were NOT reachable (eligible for archival)
6. relates_to edges are explicitly ignored in the traversal

Add CLI support: `python3 ticket-graph.py --archive-eligible [--tickets-dir=<path>]` outputs JSON array of eligible ticket IDs.

## File Impact
- Modify: `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-graph.py`
- Modify: `.test-index` (remove RED marker)

## TDD Requirement (GREEN)
Task 9af9-5f24 tests go GREEN.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `bash tests/run-all.sh` passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] `ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py` passes (exit 0)
  Verify: ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py
- [ ] Function exists
  Verify: python3 -c "import importlib.util; s=importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('g','plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-graph.py'); m=importlib.util.module_from_spec(s); s.loader.exec_module(m); assert hasattr(m,'compute_archive_eligible')"
- [ ] All eligibility tests pass GREEN
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-ticket-archive-eligibility.sh 2>&1 | grep -q 'FAILED: 0'
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed: compute_archive_eligible() implemented. All 13 eligibility tests GREEN.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
